reason=bead misfiled — target source files do not exist in this repository ## Summary This bead (fizeau-af9d5f1c) is filed in the fizeau bead queue, but the changes it prescribes belong to the DDx CLI repository (github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx), not to fizeau (github.com/easel/fizeau). An operator must move/refile the bead into the DDx repo's queue, or explicitly redirect it to patch the vendored skill snapshot under `.agents/skills/cli/` here in fizeau. ## Evidence 1. This worktree's `go.mod` declares `module github.com/easel/fizeau`. There is no top-level `cli/` directory: $ ls cli ls: cannot access 'cli': No such file or directory 2. The bead names `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:701` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go`. Neither path exists at the repository root. The only copies present in this repo are read-only skill-bundle mirrors: ./.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go ./.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go ./.claude/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go ./.claude/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go These are vendored snapshots of the DDx CLI source distributed with the DDx skills package; their `go.mod` says `module github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx`. They were last touched by commit `d49a2428 chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260508T205324-8992c1f5`, which is a generic checkpoint rather than feature work — consistent with them being a refresh-from-upstream artifact, not a maintenance target. 3. The acceptance criteria assume the DDx repo layout: AC1/AC2: `cd cli && go test -run … ./internal/agent/...` AC3:     `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` `cd cli` is not satisfiable here. The closest analogue (`cd .agents/skills/cli && go test ./internal/agent/...`) builds, but it tests vendored upstream code that fizeau does not run — fizeau invokes the `ddx` binary from PATH. Patching the vendored snapshot would not alter the behavior of the installed `ddx` binary that triggers the bug. 4. Patching the vendored snapshot would also be undone the next time the fizeau repo refreshes its skills bundle from upstream DDx, so any local change there is non-durable by design. 5. The bug itself is real and well-diagnosed (fizeau's `.gitignore` does contain `.ddx/executions/`, confirmed at line 28 of `.gitignore`). It is simply that fizeau is the *consumer* exposed by the bug, while the *fix site* is upstream DDx. ## What an operator needs to do Either: - **(preferred)** Recreate this bead in the DDx CLI repository's bead queue with the same title/description/AC, then close fizeau-af9d5f1c with a pointer to the relocated bead; or - **(only if intentional)** Amend the bead description and AC to specify patching `.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` and `.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go` here, replace `cd cli` with `cd .agents/skills/cli` in the AC, and acknowledge in the bead that the patch will be overwritten on the next skills refresh from upstream and therefore needs to be mirrored upstream regardless. Without one of these operator decisions, an agent has no scope-legal way to satisfy this bead in the fizeau worktree. ## Why `proposed` and not `open` The blocker is not retryable by re-running an agent: the source files the bead prescribes are not in this repository, and that condition will not change between attempts. A higher-power model would reach the same dead end. Only an operator can refile or rescope the bead.

rationale: status: proposed
reason: bead misfiled — target source files do not exist in this repository

## Summary

This bead (fizeau-af9d5f1c) is filed in the fizeau bead queue, but the changes it
prescribes belong to the DDx CLI repository (github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx),
not to fizeau (github.com/easel/fizeau). An operator must move/refile the bead
into the DDx repo's queue, or explicitly redirect it to patch the vendored skill
snapshot under `.agents/skills/cli/` here in fizeau.

## Evidence

1. This worktree's `go.mod` declares `module github.com/easel/fizeau`. There is
   no top-level `cli/` directory:

       $ ls cli
       ls: cannot access 'cli': No such file or directory

2. The bead names `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:701` and
   `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go`. Neither path exists at the
   repository root. The only copies present in this repo are read-only
   skill-bundle mirrors:

       ./.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go
       ./.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go
       ./.claude/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go
       ./.claude/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go

   These are vendored snapshots of the DDx CLI source distributed with the
   DDx skills package; their `go.mod` says
   `module github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx`. They were last touched by
   commit `d49a2428 chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260508T205324-8992c1f5`,
   which is a generic checkpoint rather than feature work — consistent with
   them being a refresh-from-upstream artifact, not a maintenance target.

3. The acceptance criteria assume the DDx repo layout:

       AC1/AC2: `cd cli && go test -run … ./internal/agent/...`
       AC3:     `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...`

   `cd cli` is not satisfiable here. The closest analogue
   (`cd .agents/skills/cli && go test ./internal/agent/...`) builds, but it
   tests vendored upstream code that fizeau does not run — fizeau invokes
   the `ddx` binary from PATH. Patching the vendored snapshot would not
   alter the behavior of the installed `ddx` binary that triggers the bug.

4. Patching the vendored snapshot would also be undone the next time the
   fizeau repo refreshes its skills bundle from upstream DDx, so any local
   change there is non-durable by design.

5. The bug itself is real and well-diagnosed (fizeau's `.gitignore` does
   contain `.ddx/executions/`, confirmed at line 28 of `.gitignore`). It is
   simply that fizeau is the *consumer* exposed by the bug, while the *fix
   site* is upstream DDx.

## What an operator needs to do

Either:

- **(preferred)** Recreate this bead in the DDx CLI repository's bead queue
  with the same title/description/AC, then close fizeau-af9d5f1c with a
  pointer to the relocated bead; or

- **(only if intentional)** Amend the bead description and AC to specify
  patching `.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` and
  `.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go` here, replace
  `cd cli` with `cd .agents/skills/cli` in the AC, and acknowledge in the
  bead that the patch will be overwritten on the next skills refresh from
  upstream and therefore needs to be mirrored upstream regardless.

Without one of these operator decisions, an agent has no scope-legal way
to satisfy this bead in the fizeau worktree.

## Why `proposed` and not `open`

The blocker is not retryable by re-running an agent: the source files the
bead prescribes are not in this repository, and that condition will not
change between attempts. A higher-power model would reach the same dead
end. Only an operator can refile or rescope the bead.
